verbGlossary.CLASSIFIES INTO: {source} primary classification breaks down into {target} subcategories that further refine the project's functional use within the parent type
verbGlossary.CONSTRAINS SELECTION OF: {source} attribute limits the available options for {target} based on regulatory rules, market norms, or practical compatibility
verbGlossary.DETERMINES: {source} attribute directly dictates the value of {target} with little or no ambiguity -- the relationship is definitional rather than correlational
verbGlossary.ESTABLISHES RANGE FOR: {source} attribute sets the expected benchmark range for {target} metric, defining what 'normal' looks like for that project classification
verbGlossary.ESTABLISHES BASELINE FOR: {source} attribute sets the expected baseline for {target} metric against which project performance is compared
verbGlossary.DICTATES SCOPE OF: {source} attribute determines whether {target} systems are required and at what level of complexity based on facility function
verbGlossary.MANDATES: {source} attribute creates a regulatory or contractual obligation that requires {target} on the project
verbGlossary.TRIGGERS: {source} attribute activates {target} requirement based on threshold, classification, or regulatory rule
verbGlossary.GROUPS: {source} program classification organizes multiple {target} projects under a common capital investment category
verbGlossary.RESOLVES TO: {source} raw location input is parsed or geocoded to derive the standardized {target} geographic attribute
verbGlossary.ROLLS UP TO: {source} fine-grained geography aggregates into {target} coarser geography for regional analysis and benchmarking
verbGlossary.MAPS TO: {source} location code is used to look up the applicable {target} technical classification from reference tables or geospatial data
verbGlossary.CALIBRATES REQUIREMENTS OF: {source} classification adjusts the stringency of {target} code requirements based on environmental conditions
verbGlossary.BUCKETS INTO: {source} continuous financial value is assigned to the appropriate {target} discrete range for benchmarking cohort analysis
verbGlossary.BASELINE FOR: {source} planned or contracted attribute serves as the reference point against which {target} actual attribute is compared for performance measurement
verbGlossary.DENOMINATES: {source} financial or schedule value appears in the denominator of the {target} performance metric calculation
verbGlossary.YIELDS: {source} actual outcome compared to its baseline produces the {target} performance metric as a calculated result
verbGlossary.NORMALIZES INTO: {source} raw value is divided into {target} normalized metric to enable apples-to-apples comparison across projects of different sizes
verbGlossary.SIZES: {source} attribute determines the appropriate magnitude of {target} reserve or allocation based on project scale and risk
verbGlossary.DERIVES: {source} date attribute is used to derive {target} categorical attribute through calendar lookup
verbGlossary.PAIRS WITH: {source} procurement attribute is typically selected in combination with {target} because they interact to define the commercial risk structure
verbGlossary.ENABLES: {source} attribute makes {target} approach feasible or practical -- without {source}, {target} is difficult or impossible to execute
verbGlossary.SHAPES DURATION OF: {source} attribute significantly influences the length of {target} time period by changing the sequence or overlap of pre-construction activities
verbGlossary.CONSTRAINS: {source} physical attribute limits the allowable values of {target} code classification based on building code thresholds
verbGlossary.INCORPORATES: {source} code framework includes {target} standard by reference, making its requirements mandatory for the project
verbGlossary.INFLATES: {source} project approach increases the expected value of {target} metric above normal benchmarks due to added complexity or incomplete information
verbGlossary.REDUCES: {source} construction approach lowers the expected value of {target} metric by shifting work off-site or improving efficiency
verbGlossary.PREDICTS: {source} earned value index at a given point in the project is a statistically reliable predictor of {target} final outcome metric
verbGlossary.APPROXIMATES: {source} construction phase provides a rough approximation of {target} overall progress based on typical phase-to-completion mapping
verbGlossary.CONTRIBUTES TO: {source} metric is one of the components that drives the final value of {target} outcome metric
verbGlossary.SCALES: {source} size classification proportionally increases the expected value of {target} organizational complexity metric
verbGlossary.INCREASES: {source} factor adds to the overall burden of {target} by creating more transactions, handoffs, or coordination overhead that must be managed
verbGlossary.INDICATES: {source} metric serves as a measurable signal of {target} -- higher values reveal that {target} is present and affecting the project
verbGlossary.DEGRADES: {source} factor worsens {target} by adding latency, complexity, or friction to the process that {target} depends on
verbGlossary.ERODES: {source} negative trend directly reduces {target} by consuming the margin or buffer that {target} is designed to preserve
verbGlossary.THREATENS: {source} metric at elevated levels puts {target} at risk -- if {source} is not controlled, {target} will deteriorate
verbGlossary.AMPLIFIES: {source} project approach magnifies the impact of {target} by compressing timelines or reducing the completeness of available information
verbGlossary.STRESSES: {source} approach puts pressure on {target} by requiring faster turnaround with less complete information
verbGlossary.QUANTIFIES: {source} composite metric provides a measurable value for {target} by aggregating multiple leading indicators into a single score
verbGlossary.SIGNALS: {source} performance metric provides an early warning signal about the state of {target} -- trends in {source} predict future changes in {target}
verbGlossary.MEASURES: {source} metric directly quantifies the current state of {target} as a lagging indicator of how well risk is being managed
verbGlossary.REFLECTS: {source} metric is a downstream reflection of {target} -- when {target} improves, {source} improves as a consequence
